title: Core Competencies

content.statement: Build strategy on rare, hard-to-imitate, broadly applicable internal strengths.
content.question: What rare capability could you build deeply enough that it opens multiple markets?
content.equation: Strategic Range = Core Competence × Adjacent Market Reach
content.mental_model: Trunk and branches, many products grow from one rooted competence.
content.business_lens: Identify the 1–3 capabilities that are rare, durable, and applicable to multiple products; invest in them.
content.life_lens: Develop deep skill in a few transferable areas rather than spreading thin.
sop: Filter by rarity, durability, and applicability
Direct R&D and hiring to deepen them
Use them to enter adjacent markets
content.explainer: Honda’s engine design is the classic example: motorcycles, cars, lawn mowers, generators all leveraged the same root competence. Companies that diversify on financial logic alone often fail; those that extend a core competence often win.
questions: What rare capability could you build deeply enough that it opens multiple markets?
If you had to bet the company on one capability for the next decade, which would you pick?
Which of your current products don't actually share a root capability with the others?

definitions.def: A rare, hard-to-imitate internal capability broad enough to open multiple products or markets, rather than a single skill tied to one offer.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Core Competence 8, Adjacent Market Reach 6. Score = 8 times 6 = 48 out of a possible 100. Higher means one deep capability could credibly expand into new products or markets. Lower means your strength is too narrow or too shallow to extend beyond what you're already doing.
content.closing_statement: One deep root can grow many branches.

caption:
Principle Nº 283: Core Competencies

Build strategy on rare, hard-to-imitate, broadly applicable internal strengths. Honda’s engine design is the classic example: motorcycles, cars, lawn mowers, generators all leveraged the same root competence.

Ask yourself: What rare capability could you build deeply enough that it opens multiple markets?
